library.title: The Epistolary Library
library.subtitle: Treatises in the manner of Euler, for builders who love the universe

books.title: Letters on the Universal Machine
books.subtitle: A Treatise on WebAssembly, from Transistors to Transcendence
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: From the humblest transistor to infinite fractals running in a browser sandbox. What a computer actually is, why WebAssembly had to exist, and how the chain from silicon to screen remains faithful at every layer.

books.title: Letters on the Ownership of Memory
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Rust, from Pointers to Proof
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Why every value must have an owner, why references must not outlive their referents, and how a compiler can prove the absence of an entire class of bugs that has plagued computing for fifty years.

books.title: Letters on the Sovereignty of Value
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Bitcoin, from Barter to the Lightning Network
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: From barter and clay tablets to elliptic curves and payment channels. What money actually is, why Bitcoin had to exist, and how a network of sovereign verification replaced the need to trust any single institution.

books.title: Letters on the Sovereign Application
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Progressive Web Apps, from Cache to Coronation
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: From the humblest HTTP request to the service worker's interception, from the manifest's declaration to the cache's sovereignty. What a web app actually is, why Progressive Web Apps had to exist, and how a URL becomes an icon on your home screen.

books.title: Letters on the Composition of Systems
books.subtitle: A Treatise on the Euler Framework, from Axioms to Archmage
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: The system explaining its own engine. Five general-purpose WASM modules — JSON, Store, Theme, Router, Search — composed into a framework that powers this very library. The reader who finishes understands not just the framework, but why it must be this way.

books.title: Letters on the Making of the Library
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Software Engineering, through the Lens of the Epistolary Library Itself
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: The system explaining itself. Every concept in software engineering — from files to deployment — taught through the actual code of this very library. The reader who finishes can fork the repo and build their own.

books.title: Letters on the Web of Messages
books.subtitle: A Treatise on WhatsApp, WebAssembly, and the Sovereign Network, from Drums to Digital Markets
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: From the talking drum to the Signal Protocol, from the Onitsha marketplace to Nostr relays. How WhatsApp became Africa's digital economy, why it can serve as an event bus for peer-to-peer commerce, and how WebAssembly, Lightning, and cryptographic identity can make that economy sovereign.

books.title: Letters on the Mathematics of Reality
books.subtitle: A Treatise on the Language of the Universe, from Counting to Calculus
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Euler's own domain. From the shepherd's tally to the calculus of change, from compound interest to the bell curve. Mathematics as the African builder's birthright — the language that does not lie.

books.title: Letters on the Sovereign Enterprise
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Building, from First Customer to Enduring Institution
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: From the village market to Flutterwave. Enterprise is Africa's oldest technology. The principles of value, revenue, growth, and endurance — taught from first principles through M-Pesa, Paystack, Andela, and the ancient traders of Timbuktu.

books.title: Letters on the Generative Canvas
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Creative Coding, from Pixel to Pattern, through the Lens of African Design
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Kente patterns are algorithms. Adinkra symbols are visual programs. African design has always been computational. This treatise teaches creative coding through the lens of ancestral craft — the reader creates art that runs in the browser.

books.title: Letters on the Architecture of Wealth
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Money, Markets, and the Mathematics of Prosperity
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Financial literacy is sovereignty. From M-Pesa to stock markets, from compound interest to chamas and stokvels, from budgeting to generational wealth. The African builder who understands money cannot be deceived.

books.title: Letters on the Patterns of Nature
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Systems Thinking, from Feedback Loops to Emergent Order
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: The deep patterns that connect ecology, economics, technology, and governance. Why some cities thrive. Why some businesses scale. Why some interventions backfire. Euler saw the same laws everywhere — this book makes that vision explicit.

books.title: Letters on the Architecture of Thought
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Neural Networks, from Perceptrons to Transformers
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: AI is not magic — it is mathematics applied to pattern recognition. From the single perceptron to the transformer that powers language models, taught so the African builder creates AI, not just consumes it.

books.title: Letters on the Shape of Data
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Algorithms and Data Structures, from Arrays to Red-Black Trees
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: The hydrogen atom of computer science. Sorting, searching, graphs, trees — the foundations under every program. With African examples: Kobo360 route optimization, M-Pesa network analysis, Jumia search.

books.title: Letters on the Cryptography of Trust
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Secrets, Proofs, and Digital Sovereignty
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: From XOR to zero-knowledge proofs. The mathematics of trust between strangers — digital identity, encryption, signatures. In a continent leapfrogging to digital identity, this knowledge is sovereignty.

books.title: Letters on the Rhetoric of Clarity
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Writing, Communication, and the Art of Making the Complex Transparent
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: The most undervalued skill in technology. How Euler himself wrote — and why the builder who can explain their work clearly will always outperform the one who can only code. Includes the African griot tradition and the power of proverbs.

books.title: Letters on the Governance of the Commons
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Leadership, Community, and the Architecture of Shared Stewardship
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Every shared resource needs governance. African traditions — Ubuntu, the palaver tree, age-grade systems, gacaca courts — as models for leading teams, governing open source, and building institutions that outlive their founders.

books.title: Letters on the Manufacturing of Worlds
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Making, from the Carpenter's Bench to the Rocket's Engine
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: The carpenter and the programmer are the same. Wood, metal, plastic, fabric, code — every material obeys the same universal pattern of making. From Aba's leather to Innoson's vehicles, from the jig to the CI/CD pipeline.

books.title: Letters on the Engine of Industry
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Industrial Systems, from Supply Chains to Smart Factories
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Nations that manufacture become sovereign. Supply chains, logistics, quality systems, energy infrastructure, and Industry 4.0 — the systems that make industrialization possible. Dangote, Hawassa, AfCFTA, and the African industrial renaissance.

books.title: Letters on the Power of Nature
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Electricity, from Lightning to the Grid
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Electricity was always in the lightning, always in the spinning of the Earth. From charge and circuits to solar panels and microgrids — the reader finishes able to wire a building, design a solar system, and reason about grid architecture. Africa has more energy potential than any continent.

books.title: Letters on the Sovereignty of Keys
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Identity, Custody, and the Mathematics of Self-Ownership
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Your key is your name, your signature, your passport, and your fortress gate — all in 32 bytes. From the elder's seal to HD wallets, from multisig councils to Nostr identity. The reader finishes holding their own keys with understanding, not faith.

books.title: Letters on the Lightning of Exchange
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Payment Channels, Routing, and the Velocity of Value
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: If Bitcoin is the courthouse where property is recorded, Lightning is the market where goods change hands a thousand times before anyone visits the clerk. From payment channels to Machankura — the African Lightning economy.

books.title: Letters on the Protocol of Whispers
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Relays, Events, and the Sovereign Voice
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Before the telegraph, the Yoruba dundun drums carried messages across kingdoms. Nostr is the dundun of the digital age — anyone can drum, anyone can listen, no one controls the air. From events to relays to building your own client.

books.title: Letters on the Invisible Cloak
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Privacy, Anonymity, and the Right to Transact in Silence
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Privacy is not secrecy. A merchant in Onitsha market need not announce his profit to conduct honest trade. From CoinJoin to silent payments, from Tor to zero-knowledge proofs — the mathematics of walking unseen.

books.title: Letters on the Fortress of the Self
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Sovereign Infrastructure, Nodes, and the Architecture of Independence
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: A sovereign does not rent their castle. From Bitcoin nodes to Lightning nodes to Nostr relays — running your own infrastructure on a Raspberry Pi, powered by African sunlight. The reader finishes able to build their own fortress.

books.title: Letters on the Strength of Things
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Forces, Materials, and Structures, from the Lever to the Bridge
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Why beams bend, why arches carry, why triangles are rigid. Forces, materials, stress, strain — from the lever to Great Zimbabwe's dry-stone walls. Euler's own buckling formula connects our voice to the subject.

books.title: Letters on the Chemistry of Matter
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Elements, Reactions, and the Substance of Everything
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Why steel is strong, why concrete cures, why batteries work. The maker's chemistry — from Dangote's kilns to the lithium of the Congo.

books.title: Letters on the Nourishment of Nations
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Agriculture, from Soil to Table
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: The farmer is a manufacturer whose factory is the soil. From Ethiopian teff to Ghanaian cocoa, from drip irrigation to agritech drones.

books.title: Letters on the Healing of the Body
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Health, from the Cell to the Community
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Public health for communities. The body as system, nutrition, WASH, infectious disease, maternal health, mental health. Empowering builders to heal.

books.title: Letters on the Flow of Water
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Water, from Cloud to Cup
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: 400 million Africans lack clean water. Wells, pumps, treatment, irrigation, sanitation. The builder who provides clean water saves more lives than any doctor.

books.title: Letters on the Warmth of Fire
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Heat, Engines, and the Thermal World
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: Heat, engines, refrigeration, cooking. 3 billion cook with wood. The clean cooking revolution, biogas, solar thermal — Africa's thermal energy transition.

books.title: Letters on the Map of the Earth
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Surveying, Mapping, and the Sovereignty of Land
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: 70% of African land is unregistered. Surveying, GPS, GIS, remote sensing. The builder who maps land creates wealth from nothing.

books.title: Letters on the Language of Design
books.subtitle: A Treatise on Design Thinking, from Need to Solution
books.manner: In the manner of Euler's Letters to a German Princess
books.description: How to design things people use. User research, prototyping, visual design, accessibility. M-Pesa's genius was design, not technology.
